name: Noia - Rio Tambre Loop
description: This difficult 25.7-mile (41.4 km) road cycling route near Noia offers a challenging ride with 3036 feet (925 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling in Esteiro Do Tambre offers diverse terrain, from open agricultural areas to forested mountain slopes and the unique estuarine system where the Tambre River meets the sea. The region is characterized by its complex estuarine system, featuring tranquil marshlands and intertidal flats. Cyclists can navigate routes along riverbanks and coastal areas, with opportunities for climbs into mountainous areas with native oak groves. Elevated views are available from points like Mount Tremuzo, which stands at 514 meters. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Noia loop from Noia, a 28.4 miles (45.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total elevation gain of 2,305 feet (703 m). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Torres de Oeste loop from Noia, a difficult 42.8 miles (68.9 km) path. This route offers varied scenery, including coastal sections and a total ascent of 3,679 feet (1,121 m). Local road cyclists also love the Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Negreira loop from A Ponte Nafonso, a 50.9 miles (82.0 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling in Esteiro Do Tambre is defined by riverside paths, coastal stretches, and forested hills, with routes that cater to both moderate and difficult ability levels. The region's network of no traffic road cycling routes provides options for various fitness levels. The routes in Esteiro Do Tambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from 9 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Esteiro Do Tambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no traffic road cycling routes are available in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: There are 17 dedicated no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. This includes 9 moderate routes and 8 more challenging options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Esteiro Do Tambre generally experiences a warm and humid climate with average annual temperatures above 14ºC and moderate rainfall. This makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year, though spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or heavy rain.

Q: Are there any circular no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Yes, many of the no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Noia loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 45.8 km ride, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Torres de Oeste loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 69 km with significant elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: The region offers a varied topography, perfect for road cycling. You'll encounter open agricultural areas, forested mountain slopes, and scenic routes along the estuarine system where the Tambre River meets the sea. Expect a mix of flat sections, gentle inclines, and more challenging climbs, especially on routes heading towards elevated areas like Mount Tremuzo.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the no traffic road cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several points of interest. The historic Ponte Nafonso, a medieval bridge with twenty arches, is a notable landmark. The Tambre I hydroelectric plant and its recreational area with a suspended bridge also offer an interesting stop. For panoramic views, routes that ascend towards Mount Tremuzo (514 meters) provide rewarding vistas of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly no traffic road cycling routes?
A: While there are no routes specifically rated as 'easy' in this selection, there are several moderate options that could be suitable for beginners looking for a challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14988794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Noia loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.9 km route with around 687 meters of elevation gain, offering a good introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: The no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the serene environment, the blend of rural and maritime landscapes, and the tranquility of the roads, which allow for a peaceful cycling experience away from heavy traffic.

Q: Can I find challenging no traffic road cycling routes with significant elevation?
A: Absolutely. Esteiro Do Tambre offers several difficult routes for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24643197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Negreira loop from A Ponte Nafonso&lt;/a&gt; is an 82 km route with over 1470 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife or experience nature on these routes?
A: Yes, the region is recognized as a Site of Community Importance within the Natura 2000 Network, highlighting its ecological value. Cycling along the estuarine system, riverbanks, and through forested areas offers opportunities to observe local flora and fauna, including marshlands and intertidal flats.

Q: What are the typical distances for no traffic road cycling routes in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: The routes vary in length, catering to different preferences. You can find moderate rides around 35-45 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noia Historic Town and Harbor – Noia loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt;. For longer, more challenging rides, options extend up to 82 km, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negreira – San Vicenzo loop from O Cruceiro de Roo&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Small fishing port town to the east of the Ría de Muros y Noias define this beautiful and quiet estuary. The small town dates back to the Middle Ages and has a well-preserved historic center.
Quiet road to the dam on the river Tambre. Good views of the estuary

faqs.question: What kind of fitness level is required for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Noia - Rio Tambre Return is classified as a 'Hard road ride' and requires very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 930 meters over 41.4 kilometers. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging workout.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-maintained paved surfaces. You can expect approximately 29.8 km of asphalt and 11.4 km of other paved sections, making it ideal for road bikes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: While cycling, you'll experience the diverse landscapes of Galicia, including potential coastal views, mountainous terrain, and charming villages. The route also passes by the historic Noia Historic Town and Harbor and the notable Tambre Dam.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Ruta dos ríos Vilacova e San Xusto, Roteiro de Ponte da Pedra a San Xoán do Carballoso, Camiño das Filgueiras, O Atallo, Paseo Marítimo, Rúa da Fanequeira, Camiño de Zudres, Peirao do Poxilgo, Camiño Sameiro, Camiño das Fieteiras, Camiño do Alto da Cerca, Camiño dos Lagos, and Camiño da Misa de Vilariño.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Noia - Rio Tambre Return route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, Noia, being the starting and ending point, offers various parking options within the town. It's advisable to look for public parking near the historic town center or harbor, which are convenient starting points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Galicia generally depends on avoiding the hottest summer months and the wettest winter periods. Spring and early autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and less rainfall, making them ideal for tackling this challenging route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in ZEC Esteiro do Tambre?
faqs.answer: The Noia - Rio Tambre Return route is located within the ZEC Esteiro do Tambre (Special Area of Conservation). Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on established public roads within these areas. However, always respect local regulations, stay on designated paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the Noia - Rio Tambre Return route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces in Galicia, bringing a dog along on a demanding road bike ride of this nature is not typically recommended for safety reasons, both for the cyclist and the dog. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer designed for pets and that they can handle the duration and terrain.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Noia, which offers numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs. While specific establishments directly on the route are not highlighted, you'll find ample opportunities for refreshments before and after your ride in Noia town.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and duration, it's essential to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained road bike, helmet, suitable cycling apparel, water bottles, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a fully charged phone. Consider bringing a GPS device or using the komoot app for navigation.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: As indicated by its name, "Noia - Rio Tambre Return," this is a loop route, starting and ending in Noia. This allows you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps entirely.
